text: The surgeon identifies the area at the top portion of the stomach where the laparoscopic adjustable gastric band will be placed, creating a small pouch above the band. The presence of a hiatal hernia is noted, which will be repaired to prevent reflux post-surgery.

After fixing the hernia, the surgeon creates a tunnel behind the top portion of the stomach or esophagus under direct vision. A band passing instrument is maneuvered around the stomach, and the gastric band is placed within the abdominal cavity and attached to the instrument for safe positioning.

The surgeon positions the laparoscopic adjustable gastric band around the top portion of the stomach, creating a small pouch above the band. The band is secured in place by sewing part of the stomach over it to maintain its position, as the band itself cannot be sutured directly.

The surgeon places several sutures to secure the gastric band in position, preventing it from slipping or moving. After buckling the band, the area is inspected for safety. The tubing is then brought out through a trocar site and secured to the abdominal wall with an access port for future band adjustments.
